rulings.Case Title: FRANK JONES VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DBA MUNI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 21, 2010, line 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden and no triable issue of material fact raised. The bus' "jerk" was not unusual and did not form a basis for liability. McIntosh v. Los Angeles Ry. Corp. (1936) 7 Cal 2d 90. Plaintiff admitted he could have walked around the woman's push cart if she had not moved it (Depo 91:9-92:9) The obstruction caused by the cart was not known sufficiently ahead of time for the driver to correct it. No showing that plaintiff was disabled such that the driver owed him a heightened duty requiring an audible warning or seating before movement of the bus. No showing plaintff was intoxicated or appeared intoxicated. =(302/CWW)
